novel_features.name: Portfolio Attribution
novel_features.description: See your realized P&L broken down by market category and series over any time window — answer 'did politics actually make money this quarter?' in one command.
novel_features.rationale: Joins fills + settlements + events + series in the local store; no Kalshi tool persists this data together.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs realized P&L sliced by Kalshi taxonomy — the API returns flat fills and settlements; this command joins them with the category metadata required to attribute correctly.

novel_features.name: Odds History Tracker
novel_features.description: Show how a market's yes/no price moved over time, with a sparkline rendering — built from snapshots captured by every markets sync.
novel_features.rationale: Reads the local market_price_history table that the markets sync populates on every run. Kalshi's REST API does not return historical price series.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent asks 'how did this market move' or needs price-over-time for backtesting; the API only returns current price.

novel_features.name: Win Rate Analytics
novel_features.description: Calculate your win/loss ratio, expected value, and ROI across all settled positions, optionally sliced by category.
novel_features.rationale: Joins fills × settlements in the local store; correlating outcomes with entry prices and category metadata.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent evaluates trading-strategy performance; no Kalshi tool computes this.

novel_features.name: Settlement Calendar
novel_features.description: See upcoming settlements with your positions, expected payouts, and category breakdown over the next N days.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local positions × events.expiration_time × markets to project payouts per settlement date.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to plan around upcoming settlements; the API surfaces positions and event expiries on different endpoints.

novel_features.name: Market Movers
novel_features.description: Find markets with the biggest price swings since the last sync — sorted by absolute delta or by volume change.
novel_features.rationale: Compares snapshots in market_price_history table; impossible without persistent local data.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs to surface notable market activity; the API has no 'recent movers' endpoint.

novel_features.name: Cross-Market Correlation
novel_features.description: Compute Pearson correlation of two markets' price histories — find correlated events for hedging or signal discovery.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local statistical compute on the market_price_history snapshot series for both tickers.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent investigates cross-market relationships; no API endpoint or other tool computes correlation across Kalshi markets.

novel_features.name: Exposure Analysis
novel_features.description: Break down total exposure by category with concentration warnings when any bucket exceeds a configurable risk threshold.
novel_features.rationale: Joins local positions × markets × events × series; warns when category exposure exceeds threshold.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent needs portfolio-level risk; the API doesn't aggregate exposure by Kalshi taxonomy.

novel_features.name: Read-only safe mode
novel_features.description: Global env-var/flag lock that blocks every mutating command client-side regardless of which key tier is loaded — pair with --dry-run on every mutator for safe scripting against read-only credentials.
novel_features.rationale: Generator emits --dry-run on every mutating endpoint mirror; the safe-mode env lock short-circuits before the request is signed.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent runs against an unknown Kalshi key tier — the read-only key tier exists and mid-script 403s are common; this surfaces the constraint client-side before the API is hit.

novel_features.name: Subaccount roll-up
novel_features.description: Aggregate positions, fills-today, balance, and exposure-by-category across every subaccount you can see — household view of risk and resting orders.
novel_features.rationale: Iterates the local subaccounts table, joins each subaccount's positions and balance rows, emits an aggregate view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an FCM-tier user needs aggregate risk across subaccounts; no Kalshi tool surfaces a household view.

novel_features.name: Watchlist with snapshot diffs
novel_features.description: Maintain a local watchlist of tickers; `watch diff` shows price/volume change vs the last sync per watched market — Riley's daily-snapshot ritual reduced to one command.
novel_features.rationale: Local watchlist table joined to market_price_history snapshots; pure local query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when an agent tracks a stable set of markets across runs; the API has no watchlist concept.

narrative.headline: Trade prediction markets, persist tick data, and answer category-level P&L questions Kalshi.com cannot.
narrative.value_prop: Every Kalshi feature, plus a local SQLite store that records market prices on every sync — so movers, correlations, and price history work offline. Honest about read-only vs read/write keys, with --dry-run on every mutator and a global --read-only safe-mode lock for newsroom and bot deployments.
narrative.auth_narrative: Kalshi requires composed RSA-PSS signature auth: a UUID access key id (KALSHI_API_KEY) plus an RSA private key file (KALSHI_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH or KALSHI_PRIVATE_KEY). Kalshi issues two key tiers — read-only and read/write — and the CLI honors KALSHI_READ_ONLY=1 (or --read-only) as a client-side lock that blocks every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE before signing, regardless of which tier is loaded. Write commands run against a read-only key will surface a 403 from Kalshi; pair with --dry-run while debugging.

quickstart.comment: verify your key id + private key are loaded; reports configured/source
populate the local SQLite store including the market_price_history snapshot table
browse open markets from the live API (sync first for offline filtering)
render the captured price history with an inline sparkline (requires ≥2 syncs)
compute realized P&L grouped by Kalshi category

troubleshoots.fix: export KALSHI_PRIVATE_KEY_PATH=~/.kalshi/private_key.pem (RSA PEM from Kalshi.com → Account → API Keys)

troubleshoots.symptom: Loaded a read-only key but want to script safely
troubleshoots.fix: export KALSHI_READ_ONLY=1 (or pass --read-only) — the client blocks every POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE before signing
your loaded API key is read-only. Issue a read/write key from Kalshi → Account → API Keys, or run write commands with --dry-run while debugging

troubleshoots.symptom: markets movers returns no rows or no movement
troubleshoots.fix: run kalshi-pp-cli sync at least twice an hour apart — movers reads from market_price_history snapshots
run kalshi-pp-cli account get-api-limits --json to inspect your tier; lower sync parallelism with kalshi-pp-cli sync --concurrency 2

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for kalshi-pp-cli when an agent needs price-over-time or category-level analytics on Kalshi markets — the public API only returns current prices and flat positions, so historical and aggregated questions require the local snapshot store. Use it for daily portfolio reconciliation, signal discovery via correlation across markets, and safe scripting against read-only API credentials.

recipes.title: Politics-category P&L this quarter
recipes.explanation: Realized P&L attributed to Kalshi taxonomy; --select narrows the response to the three fields agents need.

recipes.title: Track the Fed-cut market on a watchlist
recipes.command: kalshi-pp-cli watch add KXFEDFUNDS-26FEB && kalshi-pp-cli watch diff --since 24h --agent
recipes.explanation: Add ticker to local watchlist, then ask for the per-ticker delta since yesterday.

recipes.title: Correlate inflation and rate-cut markets
recipes.explanation: Pearson r computed locally over snapshot price series for both markets.

recipes.title: Safe paper-trading session
recipes.command: kalshi-pp-cli portfolio create-order --ticker KXTEST-2026 --side yes --count 1 --yes-price 50 --action buy --dry-run
recipes.explanation: Both safety floors engaged: client-side read-only lock + dry-run; never reaches the API. (Pair with KALSHI_READ_ONLY=1 to engage the client-side safe-mode lock.)

recipes.title: Movers in sports markets, last 24h
recipes.explanation: Computes price deltas from market_price_history snapshots; works offline after a sync.
